title: Triticum goiranianum
taxonomy: Triticum goiranianum is classified as a species of wild wheat. It belongs to the family Poaceae and the genus Triticum. Within the genus, it is closely related to other species like Triticum durum and Triticum aestivum. The classification of Triticum goiranianum places it as a tetraploid wheat, meaning it has four sets of chromosomes. As a wild wheat species, it is considered to be one of the ancestors of domesticated wheat species.
uses: Triticum goiranianum, also known as Goiran's wheat, is a species of wild wheat native to the Mediterranean region. Unfortunately, there is limited information available on its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to determine any potential health benefits associated with this plant species. Traditional knowledge from local communities may hold some secrets about the plant's uses, but these are not well-documented or scientifically proven. More studies are required to fully understand the medicinal properties of Triticum goiranianum.
